ddls:
CREATE TABLE "demand_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded demand data */,
  "actual_demand" REAL /* Actual demand in megawatts */,
  "forecast_demand" REAL /* Forecasted demand in megawatts */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the data was collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the time of data collection */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity at the time of data collection */,
  "weather_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather at the time of data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demand_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecasting model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecasting model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., linear, ARIMA, LSTM) */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast result */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demand data entry that the forecast is based on */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model used */,
  "forecasted_value" REAL /* Forecasted value in megawatts */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date when the forecast was generated */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_results_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES demand_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_results_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for authentication */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., login, data update) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed by the user (e.g., demand data, forecast results) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the weather station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station that collected the data */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the weather data was collected */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature at the time of data collection */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Humidity at the time of data collection */,
  "weather_description" TEXT /* Description of the weather at the time of data collection */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model */,
  "performance_metric" TEXT /* Performance metric (e.g., MAE, RMSE, MAPE) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id", "performance_metric")
)
